channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: /Biz revival — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
/Biz revival

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:48 UTC

OP

while this board is clearly dead and all we have left is 10 anons, one of which is a aid devastated bald whiny fag, i think this is the perfect time to try to revive this board.

"How"? i'm hearing you ask.
Well. Let's make this a general where we only share our best picks. NO pointless SHILLING, either stocks or the occasional good crypto you really believe in.


I'll start.

Litecoin is now around 50$. This is a generational bottom if you look on the multi year chart. A solid support. At this price, unless you're convinced altcoins and cryptos are over, it's a free lottery ticket. In the same spirit, LITS, a company which treasury is only composed of its litecoin holding is also a great way to get litecoin exposure.

Ton, the crypto of one if not the most used chatting app worldwide is going back to a historical bottom as we speak after doing a small pump following several changes and pavel durov's statement. (20% staking apy, many other changes planned i won't go into details but good narrative)

A risky stock play but with very good risk / reward ratio: UBISOFT, company went to shit, but they are restructuring and trying to turn things around. A potential 10 bagger over the next years if they can achieve that, and they definitively can if they stop their bullshit.

Early replies (first 3)

62265475
>>62265467
Just buy OpenAI at IPO.
All normalfags will do that and outperform your "smart" plays.
There's such a thing as being too smart.

62265476
>>62265474
yeah that's precisely what we're trying to avoid you fucking retard. Stay poor

62265480
but what if I am bald?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265481
>>62265475
so you think buying openAI at its ipo is going to bring major returns? arent' we at the top of the AI Bubble

62265483
>the next rubic

62265484
>>62265467
cool idea, goodluck with the thread
here's mine i guess: certain tokenized community that's been having more and more in person conferences, like early BTC
but believe im not delusional enough to actually think shilling anything here will do anything, im just making conversation

62265486
>>62265481
Being contrarian usually doesn't pan out.
Just stop thinking and left curve it.

62265490
Carbon Credits

62265494
>>62265467
UBISOFT leaked my data and I almost lost my e-mails. It's been making basically the same game for over 20 years for the same braindead public and every multiplayer game has stupid broken mechanics and lacks balance. I hope it goes bankrupt and its CEO hangs himself.

62265497
>>62265490
KraneShares Global Carbon Strategy ETF

62265498
>>62265476
just because you didin't research, doesn't mean its bad pick. show me another meme, that have more to offer than cheese. they keep build shit, and you missing it for litecoin. kek

62265500
>>62265497
>KraneShares Global Carbon Strategy ETF
interesting looking into it. Im kinda skeptical about the whole concept of carbon credits tho

62265502
>>62265500
It's the Future, everything will be build around it, every single Produkt every single company, every city and every country.

62265512
>>62265502
Nah, read the room.
The ESG thing is actually going away.
It was always a forced meme.

62265524
>>62265512
It hasn't even begun

62265525
>>62265467
The problem of your picks, at least for LTC and UBI (I don’t know anything about TON), is that they have no moat and their lunch is being eaten by newer and/or bigger rivals. They will probably bounce back at some point, but how high and after how many extra losses? And it’s not like these assets will be more resilient than others if a market downturn comes, they will probably sink even harder than the rest. Your capital could probably be allocated on better assets…

Cryptos with moat are BTC (the Coca-Cola of cryptos), the privacy-oriented ones (XMR and more dubiously ZEC) and the DeFi-oriented ones (which is promising for KAS whose upcoming Toccata hard fork will enable DeFi applications).

I wouldn’t touch any software company, including video games companies, because of the current AI frenzy and inflationary pressures on the consumer discretionary sector. That said, I’d rather hold any of the top 20 video games companies rather than UBI where you can’t say people wait for the next Ubisoft game like they wait for GTA VI or the next Mario. Overall I’d rather invest in RBLX or U.

62265535
>>62265486
>Just stop thinking and left curve it
Top signal. You’re going to learn the hard way that being thoughtful with your own money is important.

item.title: 16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas — 26 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:03 UTC

OP

Two pizzas aren’t on the table; they’re sitting at it. This was a deliberate act of rub, clearly signalling to the right crowd. BTW, where are these kids now? Because I can find his recent photos, but zero photos of *his* kids.

Early replies (first 3)

62261169
>>62261166
Still more valuable than anything else bitcoin has ever done

62261183
Why isn’t this diverse

62261194
>>62261183
ew

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261207
>>62261166
haha yeah wouldn't that be funny. lucky me even back then it was impossibly hard to mine so i have none

62261263
>>62261166
bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck, at that age parents get a picture or two a year if lucky. less if they live out of town.

62261274
>>62261263
That dad is 100% gay you can see it on his face and his faggy kids, similar to that jewish actor Sabretooth whos son was sucking on his bike handle like it was a sucker, now he's a famous trans femboy.

62261301
>>62261166
>2010: 10,000 BTC can buy two pizzas
>2026: 10,000 BTC can buy two nationwide pizza chains

62261391
>>62261166
Looks like what I ordered on a date last week, I said to her "What are you ordering?" LOL

62261396
>>62261166
>10,000 bitcoin for two pizza's
>creepy pic with two kids as evidence
Yeah, sure, and John Podesta has a diverse collection of handkerchiefs, righto

62261400
>>62261166
Bitcoin wouldn't be where its out without that purchase

62261517
>>62261263
>bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck
Alright, where is his wife then? There's zero photos of him and mother of *his* kids

62261678
>>62261391
Nice one Saul

62261685
>>62261166
child trafficking right there in front of everyone all in the name of monetary freedom

62261687
>>62261517
Not everyone broadcasts their life's online you zoomer fuck. There are literally zero images of me on the internet outside of some old Facebook friends from 10 years ago and I have been online since 95

62262003
10,000 BTC is currently worth $771,584,132 USD
Ouch. So you're telling me he spent nearly a billion on two crappy pizzas? I hope he kept some and didn't blow his full stack on this purchase. Does anyone know if he's worth millions today?

62262910
>>62261274
>>62261166
why are you such a massive faggots? you are stinky ALTers, aren't you.
fuck off, losers

62263805
>>62261166
wtf is the point of posting this stupid ass shit like this over and over again. like we get that bitcoin was worthless than a penny. are you trying to get people to buy into bitcoin to get that same multiplier. "be a good little moon boy, don't do anything useful or productive, dont figure shit out. buy moon coin and watch the line go up and down like a good little boy. don't forget to wonder who Satoshi Nakamoto is every once an awhile to switch things up." literally go die

62263868
>>62262003
he said he has a few btc left supposedly.
RIP

62263880
>>62263868
also R.I.P those bois buttholes.
>>62261396
>>62261685

62264105
>>62261263
>16 years ago
>those kids are +20 years old now

62264470
>>62261166
This pic is sus af.

62264492
>>62261166
He paid using a payment method that wasn't recognized yet and still isn't or did he give them to some other guy who paid for it?

62264689
>>62262003
Btc literally would not exist as it does today without him. Fucking zoomies are impossibly stupid.

62264729
>>62261166
So you want me to believe this guy wasted almost a billion dollars sacrificing these two kids to Moloch?

62265428
>>62264105
>>16 years ago
>>those kids are +20 years old now
>>lol what an idiot
>>(I do not understand that they were >0 age when taking this pic)

62265462
>>62264729
All I’m saying is this was probably staged, and used to signal to the (((right group))) of people what you can do with Bitcoin and mock normies who are oblivious.
>Bitcoin, two kids and cheese pizza on the table
Yeah, right.

item.title: This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled. — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:36 UTC

OP

This stock is literally rat poison quadrupled.

No reality based in anything, just complete emotion.

-7% for a week while it smashed earnings, meanwhile everything else is shooting up like crazy 4-5% gains EVERY SINGLE DAY.

AMD 1M is up 54%
Nvidia 1M is up 7%. Literally poison.

Early replies (first 3)

62264735
>>62264699
What do you expect out of a stock that's up 1300% over 5 years and already holds the #1 spot by marketcap

62264762
>>62264699
I sold Nvidia when it hit $220 a few weeks ago. The past few quarters earnings report made me realize it's the most heavily manipulated stock right now. Only institutional investors decide when it dances

62265151
>>62264735
>holds the #1 spot by marketcap
this isn't a real reason tho
>"well, good work everyone, but we have #1 marketcap, time to stop growing!"
it's a spook, a fake constraint that sounds to people who don't understand the core ideas behind the economy (ie. the point of all of this)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265162
>>62264735
The problem with being #1 is that there's only 1 direction left to go.

62265231
>>62264699
+64% 1Y is bad?

62265520
>>62264699
Your entitlement is absurd

item.title: A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn… — 13 replies — 22.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:14 UTC

OP

A few weeks ago, an anon posted that if Chainlink succeeds and everything goes as planned in the future, you could earn $1 per 1 LINK per DAY through staking. Does that mean if I’ve staked 2000 LINK, I’d earn $2000 per day?
Is that true, or was the anon just talking bullshit?

Early replies (first 3)

62258684
complete bullshit. if they opened staking to everyone and diverted 100% of all revenue to stakers(never happening), each link would make you less than a penny a day.

good thing holders are retarded and you can just make up bullshit for a decade tho

62258761
yes, but a loaf of bread will also cost 2000

62259325
test

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259833
>>62258598
>sergei will deny again

62259895
>>62258598
2 in the pink, 1 in the LINK

62261509
>>62258598
Yes my friend your math is not a challenge. It is to be this for us and to every one of our communities so good and wealth will be extreme. We will to be in good spirits and also very full with lots of food and enjoyment for our families and lives! Go go gadget LINK!!!!

62262042
Please be honest with me, bros... no jokes, no meme answers, no shitposts, just 100% honesty. What price will Chainlink realistically go to?

62262203
>>62262042
3,5 - 4 $

62262214
>>62262042
It is to be happening with you and me both my brother very very soon with much haste and wealth for good lifes!!!! We will stand up like Iron man next to Thor and cry victory!

62264939
>>62262042
$8

62265165
>>62262042
llm models will say 80-100 in a couple years

62265171
>>62265165
llm models are not fortune tellers, stinkie

62265491
>>62265165
>cuz the ai just knows stuff

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:21 UTC

OP

Linkies officially roundtripped 9 years of gains against Bitcoin.

Congrats on this, seriously

62265402
At this point, I'd rather buy Trump memecoin than Link. Seriously

62265403
>>62265398
Seems like a good time to slurp

62265410
>>62265403
>buying a rugged 2017 shitcoin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265415
>>62265398
damn this is absolutely brutal, officially no gains vs. bitcoin.

62265425
>>62265403
This. Dont underestimate the power of positive thinking

62265445
>>62265398
Works on my machine.

62265450
>>62265445
>log chart
>still looks atrocious

62265487
>>62265450
LINK outperformed BTC.

62265501

why didn't you sell in 2020?

item.title: Make money with STEM degree! How is it possible? Anyone who is STEM and make it big? — 9 replies — 10.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Make money with STEM degree! How is it possible? Anyone who is STEM and make it big?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-05-23 09:56 UTC

OP

Make money with STEM degree!
How is it possible?
Anyone who is STEM and make it big?

Early replies (first 3)

62264713
what's your idea of big? i fucked around completely until I was 26 or so, got a CS degree, 40 now and my networth is about 3.5m which includes a paid off house

62264717
>>62264704
>>62264713
so bullish at tech

62264731
>>62264704
>>62264713
How do you fuck around and then get a CS degree? Isn't having an high IQ a requirement? Idk how you waste right years and then jump back into college doing high level maths

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264742
>>62264731
the hardest math class i took was a calc based linear algebra, it was pretty hard but i just made myself do it. I got like a B- or something in that class, it was enough to satisfy the requirement. most of the curriculum was learning how computer systems work and programming assignments.

i don't know, i think being older was a big advantage because i was dead set clear about why i was there and what i wanted to accomplish, vs. a bunch of retard immature kids who have no clue why they're even there or what they want to do. 26 isn't that old really, your brain isn't mush yet even though i'd basically been doing weed, shrooms and playing WoW full time for the past several years. I actually had a bit of a drinking problem - high functioning alcoholic but I've kicked that by now.

62264766
>>62264704
>>Make money with STEM degree!
>in 2026
a STEM degree will only make you poorer in 2026

62264773
>>62264713
Nice larp bro

>>62264766
Can confirm. Wish I had studied something boring like accounting.

62264781
>>62264704
Yes, I'm a civil engineer and I make a whopping... $95k/yr!

62264783
>>62264731
>Isn't having an high IQ a requirement?
kek, what? The average IQ of a CS student is like 120. If your IQ is below 120 you might as well go weld or something

62265449
>>62264781
Are you bald and fat and sleepless.

item.title: What if wagies have it figured out? I used to think the NEET lifestyle was for losers, but now I'm wondering if my minds… — 4 replies — 9.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
What if wagies have it figured out? I used to think the NEET lifestyle was for losers, but now I'm wondering if my minds…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:25 UTC

OP

What if wagies have it figured out? I used to think the NEET lifestyle was for losers, but now I'm wondering if my mindset is outdated. I know a few NEETs in their 20s and 30s and they all have so, so, so much more more time and energy than me. Most of them will inherit houses, and one of them has alluded to scamming the government for gibs and putting these in index funds. I suspect these people have more saved up than I do. All of them have learned random skills, some of which they can probably monetize if they need to get a job (I don't have time or energy to learn stuff, so ironically I might be less employable than these people if my current skillset becomes obselete). Also, these NEETs have consumed more leisure time than I will probably EVER consume in my entire life. I'm starting to get depressed and angry that I followed "the rules" when clearly they don't apply anymore. They're just boomer myths. Obviously there are NEETs in hellish lives and wagies in great lives, but as a general rule I think I've misjudged who has it better. Am I missing something here?

Early replies (first 3)

62265059
>>62265056
just chill. you don't need to consume so much you can work less and enjoy life more. see you can have all the money in the world but whats the point if you never get to enjoy it

62265245
let the wagies deal in the real world

62265323
>>62265056
>get capital and invest
>retire early with money
That's the best option for many people, rather than just being a neet or just being a wagie.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62265504
>>62265323
most fail at the get capital part and get stuck as slaves forgotten as soon as they stop being useful

item.title: Best paying jobs in NYC — 10 replies — 8.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Best paying jobs in NYC

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-05-23 09:39 UTC

OP

I’m trying to figure out realistic career options in NYC that don’t require college.
What are the highest paying jobs you can get with just a high school diploma (or maybe short certifications/training)?

Looking for stuff that actually pays well long term

Early replies (first 3)

62263408
>>62263399
that is gonna be hard... high paying without college is really not easy especially in NYC. Maybe garbage man?

62263451
>>62263399
The stock market pays here more than any job outside of the highest-end medical or engineering ones here, unless you count the amount you can steal through government favors.

62263469
>>62263451
The Stock market?
and how can i possibly learn? (or sell my soul)

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263479
>>62263399
Run a Waste Management company

62263611
>>62263479
>>62263479
that's jersey

62263618
>>62263399
Any "job" will be a dead end, you can get a job to learn what’s happening in a specific industry but then you have to get out ASAP

Then, start your own company and sell high-end goods or services, like, if it’s a food truck, it has to be a luxury food truck with exclusive ingredients and esthetic looks, if you’re selling umbrellas they have to look incredible and be hand-made in NYC, etc.

NYC is the place to be in the luxury segment, and it’s where entrepreneurs without academic credentials can shine while making huge margins on whatever they sell

Good luck!

62263673
>>62263399
most likely a paid protestor/agitator for propaganda events

62263675
>>62263399
>highest paying jobs you can get with just a high school diploma
subway janitor (unionized)
unironically

62263847
>>62263399
Run for office. About 100 years of new york mayors and governors scamming the taxpayers proves this.

62265418
>>62263479
Anyway you got a problem, that money from Kekistan, they gave me all types of grief about it. Said they have a new finger printing technique can lift the prints right off the bills.

62265537
>>62265528

There’s no point cause they won’t hire you anyway

>tfw L*nK will be worth 15 whole dollarinos in the 2030 bear market
good thing I have a 100k suistack desu

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 218 replies — 89.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 218 • Updated: 2026-05-23 08:23 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263815
what's so great about kids? The amount of propaganda lately is crazy.

62263849
>>62263797
I’ll never understand the whole dink thing. just pointless to get married if you don’t plan on having kids.

62263988
>>62263605
>ask for specifics
>get vague references
>screenshot from fucking Australia
Kill yourself nigger
>Or any other million of scenarios where the pre-nup gets nullified in court.
Then show us two of them

62263992
>>62263988
you can't write a contract that violates the law.

so any time a prenup disagrees with divorce law, the prenup gets thrown out. It happens all the time.

62264003
>>62263428
>$10k or $100k
These are not the same.
But I agree with the sentiment. There's points in life where you gotta wake up and get your shit together.
>>62263605
Common Law Marriage was the most insane fucking thing when I first heard of it. In some states, if you've been dating a girl for 10 years, you legally end up considered married and have to pay shit like alimony if you break up.
It's the sort of thing that sounds like it was thought up in the 1800's, but nobody's repealed it.

62264007
>>62263653
The third person in the threesome is also another guy.

62264012
>>62263988
Not my fault you’re a retard. Why would I waste my time spoonfeeding a retard? You’re an incel anyways. Why do you care about prenups? You’ve never had sex.

62264027
>>62264003
>Common Law Marriage was the most insane fucking thing when I first heard of it. In some states, if you've been dating a girl for 10 years, you legally end up considered married and have to pay shit like alimony if you break up.
Yep, people don’t know how fucked up family law is until it starts dickslapping them in the face. What’s even funnier is when you find out that your 12 year old son actually isn’t biologically yours, but, for whatever myriad of reasons, the court doesn’t care and you gotta pay child support anyways. For a lot of states in a lot of circumstances, it actually doesn’t matter if “your” son came out of your balls or not. Your wife fucked the mailman and got caught? Sorry buddy, but that boy is still legally yours cuz reasons.

62264028
>>62259935
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing.

Why not?
>

62264045
>>62263557
Why would he be a deadbeat? If he leaves, he can send them like 300 dollars a month and they will be okay. Also pay for their college, etc

62264297
>>62263178
why do people just larp and completely invent stories on this site? is it some kind of attention seeking thing from an unresolved toddler need?

truly disgusting lot on this board. i think i won't be coming back.

62264345
>>62259935
sorry, your thread has been noticed

62264378
>>62259935
This is not the person I would mock, he actually did fucking make it. I have no idea how you get 12 years in work make near 2 million and also a fully paid house jesus christ wow.

You should mock me, I was 39, decided I wasn't wasting my 40s in a deadend job but I had 400K saved up and just said fuck it tried to invest in dividend stocks and live in my parents house they kicked me out so I tried to day trade with 200K and seven years later I am finally broke and need a job but I got a super shitty resume and no recent job history or references who remember who I am.

I am near 50 and am pretty fucked. Trying to not kill myself. Might do a bootcamp for tech with last 10K or go try a masters or do the grind Walmart -- Office -- go back to banking.

62264379
>>62264045
the most important thing about a dad is spending time with the kids, not sending 300 a month. The girl will be a whore and son turn trans without that presence. Look at Elon's kid.

62264434
>>62264379
> the most important thing about a dad is spending time with the kids
Not true. Grew up with really rich kids. Money is more important. Only brokie fathers think time with kids is more important.

62264547
>>62263992
Yeah. But you absolutely CAN structure a legal one that lets you split premarital assets. That’s been my whole point. You’re comparing apples and railroad ties.

62264551
>>62264012
>still no proof
Keep going. This is fun.
>never had sex
Woman argument. You’ve lost and it’s clear.

62264565
>>62264379
The kid will have their mom's parents, grandparents, aunt and uncles, etc. That stuff can happen regardless, even in the West with two loving parents. And he can stay in the country and visit them from time to time if he really wants.

62264595
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing.
Kek, why not? I'm on the same exact path. Life is extremely cozy. Fuck this faggot society

62264600
>>62259968
>Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.
what kind of faggot do you have to be to unironically write this shit? If you even consider getting married in le current year, you are a wheelchair bound, drool tier fucking retard.

62264605
>>62260997
based

62264672
>>62264595
You’re supposed to slave away like everyone else.
lol.
“Society” is like 95% worker bees with no choice but to toll.

62264833
>>62259935
I dislike people cooking for me. I don't know how I will be able to voice this to my future wife

62264838
>Aw geez guys I have 76 years of spending saved up at 35 years old do I have enough to retire?
Uhh, yes. Congrats. Although he'd be better off putting most of it in a trust with a reasonable $40k/yr drawdown and pursue marriage as a trustfund kid, and not have to worry about divorce after that.

62264839
>>62260039
>>62262037
These comics were highly respected in the olden days. Remember this one?

62264927
>>62259968
Marry a rich bride, retard. Fucking low class roastoid marrying nigger, just kill yourself loser.

62264930
>>62259980
Where do all you currycels come from? Unbelievable.

62265011
>>62260025
Surrogacy. Be a single dad.

62265250
>>62259935
>You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s

62265283
>>62259968
>marry
>because one divorce
Then don't marry?
>muh keedz
You can have them without marrying
>muh common law
Move somewhere where that's not a thing, or recycle through women.
>>62259993
>monk
You can enjoy life without living paycheck to paycheck. Sex is basically free if you find the right girl. Packing homemade food and eating somewhere new every weekend costs less than fast food.

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 136 replies — 61.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 136 • Updated: 2026-05-23 08:37 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264078
>>62263931
You won't afford a house just because you stop eating chips. They are lying to you.

62264089
>>62262449
Fast food is a gigantic grift now.
They're draining what they can from low impulse control / high time preference slobs while their business model still works.
Unless you're on the road, there's no excuse for fast food anymore. It doesn't taste good, it's not healthy, and it's the same price as a normal takeout joint.

That said, the restaurant industry as a whole is a status grift for women. You don't even get good service anywhere short of fine dining. The demand is for people to say they went to X and snap a few selfies/food pictures, not to actually enjoy the meal together and hang out with their group.
I can't remember the last time I actually finished a plate before the waitstaff was trying to push the bill on us.

62264122
Caleb is fat therefore his opinion/advice is discarded

62264124
>MAGA is telling you to eat like a slave so we can continue funding wars for Israel
>the same people that squealed like pigs a few years ago because eggs were pricey

62264174
>just eat poisonous fake food goyslop, goyim
>real food is reserved for us, not you

62264324
>>62263155
Yes its especially important while developing.

62264336
>>62264324
It's about humiliation. My expensive food isn't as good unless I make you eat garbage.

62264558
>>62262607
I agree with you anon. Boomers are boomers, don’t need to beat that dead horse. But routinely eating out is retarded on many levels.

62264577
>>62264124
this lol
fucking pathetic

62264580
>>62263206
Post body fatass

62264583
>>62262454
I love Dave Ramsey. Even more than loving him, I love when poor, stupid zoomers talk about how he's a retard and doesn't know anything. Then I ask them how a retard boomer like Dave becomes worth hundreds of millions of dollars while they can't afford a running car.

62264663
>>62262607
I think my dad was 19 years old when the gold standard was abandoned. Crazy what boomers got away with.

62264675
yes, you deserve to have fast food delivered to your door. yes, you deserve to have it chewed and dropped into your mouth.

62264691
>>62264583
I can afford a running car doe

62264733
>>62264583
>you being born into a dystopian hellscape economy is the same as being born a boomer with daddy's money backing him during the most prosperous time in human history

nice bait

62264944
>>62264089
> Unless you're on the road, there's no excuse for fast food anymore. It doesn't taste good, it's not healthy, and it's the same price as a normal takeout joint.
This. You can get to go meals from a real restaurant for like $2 more if you don’t feel like cooking or whatever. Fast food isn’t a value at all. It’s a necessity when you don’t have other options. Or if you’re retarded

62264946
>>62264122
Based

62264980
>>62262862
That's common here in New Zealand as well.
I've seen people turn up with;

A whole cooked chicken and nothing else.
A 12 pack of bread rolls and a tin of Tuna.
One large bag of carrots.
A tin of Baked Beans and no can opener.
A loaf of bread and a smoked chicken breast.
Half a dozen cooked snags.
Plain rice and a chicken drumstick.
Two Sea Urchins.
A cheesy loaf.
A banana bunch.

Last nights dinner is very common.

62265020
>>62262598
you can put a loaf of bread in the fridge to make it last longer, anon

62265030
>>62263602

62265033
>>62263530
they are too retarded to understand that the shiny rocks are what go into their computers to design and make their pokemon cards

62265037
>>62265020
depends on conditions, more important for bread is air tightness and moister.. putting it in some fridges can make it go faster depending.. you can freeze bread and defrost it as you need pretty fast though

62265043
>>62265037
For toast putting the bread in the freezer is just sensible.

62265138
>>62265020
Just stick it in a bread bin like a normal person

62265148
>>62262449
boomer here

I was 30 years old when I could afford to eat at a restaurant for anything other than a special occasion. I was 40 when I could afford to eat at a restaurant whenever I wanted.

younger generations don't get a lot of pity for demanding luxuries we worked our asses off for and they think are just rights every person deserves.

same with houses. And new cars. Earn it. It took us decades of work.

62265153
>>62265138
Look at this fancy fuck with his bread humidor.

62265271
I'm bringing bread and cheese and ham and some fucking canned soup with me to Stockholm next week to beat the Swedish jew and there's nothing you can do to stop me. And some booze in my checked bag. Buying alcohol in Sweden will bankrupt a millionaire.

62265280
>>62262630
To be honest you can make bread on the griddle, so the bread maker is just wasted money.

62265288
>>62265271
Buy Felix' Gulasch when you're there. It is a yummy soup.

62265303
>he doesn’t make his own bread

OP

If you still don’t own any Bitcoin yet, anon what the hell are you waiting for?

>Banks give you tiny interest while inflation slowly destroys your purchasing power anyway. I’d rather have BTC long term than let my money rot in a savings account.

Early replies (first 3)

62245757
i get 5btc later for cheaper but thanks for the advice!

62245760
>>62245741
Too eqrly but yeah and avoid cex or at least take it out as fast as u can!

62246438
>>62245757

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62258577
>>62257680
you have zero bitcoin

62258754
>>62257223
Where is he now

62258775
>>62257679
nonsense. It's all in your head

62258793
>>62258754

62259397
>>62245757
lets hold hands and buy in Oct

62259745
>>62259397

62260090
>>62259745
He’s buying

62261050
>>62257679
BTC infrastructure is basically trying to solve that balance now: preserving self custody while making native BTC more usable across broader financial systems. Maybe you’re just not updated yet.

62261057
>>62252472
Buy btc for many reasons anon.

62261131
>>62257675

62261260
>>62261050
It's kinda scary to go deeper with that
>>62261131
Plus big chance for theft

62261264
>>62258793
Hello I'm here

62261340
>>62257392

62261342
>>62252488

62261452
>>62260090
100k by October

62261462
>>62261452
no chance
this will count as the summer of the big dump

62261476
>>62261462
Your opinion doesn't matter to me

62261608
>>62261340
Why do people still not know about this?
Better to explore and understand the TBV direction early before launch.

62261618
>>62261462

62261632
>>62252488

62261641
>>62261131

62262278

62262820
>>62261608

62262829

62262834

62262852

62262893

item.title: >*predicts the price of BTC in your path* — 22 replies — 36.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
>*predicts the price of BTC in your path*

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-05-23 10:13 UTC

OP

>*predicts the price of BTC in your path*

Early replies (first 3)

62263829
Well, except for today, wtf

You're supposed to go up today, you stupid coin

62264034
>>62261213
literal meme lines

62264038
>>62263829
>trick algos into thinking the price will go up so they buy
>dump into them with size
thanks for playing!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62264048
>>62264034
I promise I'm working on a 85-IQ friendly version

62264064
>it doesnt work
>im making an 85 iq version
good you can finally pretend to understand

62264083
>>62264064
>78% hit rate since 2024
>nails trend for 2 months
>one date of noise (so far)
>"it doesn't work"

62264123
>>62264083
>youre still poor
?

62264146
>>62264123
>quoting me calling you something I only strongly implied

62264150
>>62264146
>you're still poor
?

62264159
>>62264150
>quoting me calling you something I only strongly implied

62264163
>>62261213
When and at what price will the next 2 year low be

62264178
>>62264163
No idea. The outputs are based on a physics simulation that predicts likely market pivot points, and the output starts to blur about 5 weeks out so I run it in 4-week chunks. "Buy on green arrows/sell on red arrows/derisk on yellow diamonds" has been pretty reliable, especially when you add liquidity-based confirmation/invalidation (red x's or green checks)

Historically speaking? Early-mid June

62264197
>>62264178
>”I have no idea what I’m talking about and my indicator is unreliable at best!”

Nice blog.

62264209
>>62264197
>consistent ~80% hitrate since 2024, including through the war in which random Presidential tweets can knock 5% off orange coin's value
>unreliable

62264602
>>62264209
Wow so 80 percent of the time it works all the time? Shut up and take my money!!!!1!!!

62264608
>>62263829
they operate on a higher level, they have all the obvious models and they play against them

62264640
>>62261213
So double X is confirmation? Looks good but don't you keep getting buy signals all the way down?

>>62264209
This can still be valuable as a tool for high income people who just average down.

62264678
>>62264640
>Looks good but don't you keep getting buy signals all the way down?
The system generates 4 weeks of likely buy/sell points using a schizo physics simulation, then close to the date confirms or invalidates them (X or check) using 2 basic liquidity momentum indicators. This month's chart, for example, was generated May 4. The combined physics-liquidity system predicts market pivots with about 80% accuracy, which is insane for something forecasting that far out. The demo strategy that just trades BTC once or twice a week with 1x lev is up ~700% since 2024

The lighter colored triangles are hybrid liquidity-physics signals. Getting close to a predicted buy/sell point (48 hours before), strong momentum on the liquidity side can advance or delay a buy/sell point by 24 hours. Strong bullish liquidity advances buys and delays sells, strong bearish liquidity advances sells and delays buys. Usually when you get a string of hybrid-physics signals like that, it's smart to layer in orders on each day and get heavier toward the end of the range.

The yellow diamonds are points where you get sudden "surprise" accelerations or trend reversals. The model is really good at picking them out, if you look at the chart.

Of course, sucks to be me since it looks like I've posted here right before the system got it wrong lol

62264688
>>62264678
So are you running it in production or just simulating it? Good job bro, this is cool.

62264707
>>62264688
>So are you running it in production or just simulating it? Good job bro, this is cool.
Thanks Anon! It's in production, web dashboard with live indicators and history back to December 2024, and I've been posting on Twitter since last October. It's taken a few years to develop and tune the model.

There are lots of physics-based trade models out there, modeling demand as particle density and stuff like that, but as far as I can tell this is the only public one that can predict market pivots weeks in advance. I have noticed that about 90% of the time, a predicted buy/sell that falls on a weekend gets frontrun on the last trading day before, which makes me suspect some institutions have figured it out. Which, now that I think about it, should've included the upcoming May 25th Sell being on Memorial Day

62265317
Okay, and can I use it?

62265489
>>62264608
Bog and Magog cannot be defeated

item.title: why does this keep happening — 35 replies — 23.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
why does this keep happening

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-05-23 09:21 UTC

OP

>Link pumps to $10
>BTC instantly takes a nosedive

Why is Link not allowed to go above $10?

Early replies (first 3)

62263473
because fuck you, stinkie
it really all boils down to that
the entire crypto market exists JUST TO FUCK YOU STINKIES OVER
how does that make you feel
it makes me feel pic related

62263519
>>62263473
hey it's the dick guy

62263588
But Sirgay said they've decoupled from crypto prices

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62263816
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62263870
>>62263230
Take your meds, its literally just following the btc chart. All the alt charts are the same.
Btc pumps = alts pump
Btc dumps = alts dump

Idk why you retards think link is the one causing btc to pump and dump when its the other way around

62264096
>>62263870
Sure thing rabbi

62264135
Coinbase constantly has positive notifications sent out for link, but the price is always shidding and farding.
For example: pic related

62264172
>>62263870
>ts literally just following the btc chart.
Bitcoin was crabbing, and the second Link pumped and hit $10, Bitcoin dumped.

Are you blind.

62264180
>>62264172
sweetie you forgot your medicine :}

62264354
>>62263230
because you're retarded enough to think that BTC is dumping because of LINK's movement rather than LINK is dumping because of BTC's movement.

62264389
>>62263473
why does this bitch have such a pointy schnoz, is she a kike?
t. link investoor (newfag)

62264394
>>62263870
>Btc pumps = alts pump
>Btc dumps = alts dump
More like
Btc pumps = alts dump
Btc dumps = alts dump
simple as

62264410
>>62264354
No he understands that BTC dumps, to drag link down, before it pumps too high

Just DCA anons, they'll unleash it when they're ready

62264427
>>62264410
Wrong you dumb link nigger. EVERYTHING dumps when BTC dumps. Every single coin has TA levels called resistances and supports. Shitcoins like LINK won't break its resistance without permission from BTC (permission being BTC breaking through its own resistance level) so LINK will just sit at resistance waiting on BTC's permission to go higher. If BTC dumps then LINK will dump without ever breaking the current resistance because it is a shitcoin and BTC controls movement.

Your inability to understand this is why you're poor and still bagholding absolute garbage after all these years. Hope this helps.

62264474
>>62264427
retard

62264483
>>62264474
stay poor bagholder

62264512
>>62264474
we'll never let u live it down stinker
you lost

62264795
>>62264172
Btc spiking is exactly what caused link to go to $10 dumbfuck

62264817
We will dump BTC
We will turn hanta into covid 2.0
We will pop the AI bubble
We will start WW3
We will do whatever it takes, and there's nothing you can do about it linkie

62264876
>>62263230
>Link pumps to $10
>pumps
Does anyone else remember when linkies could've exited for $30+?

62264877
>>62264427
>EVERYTHING dumps when BTC dumps.
That's not responding the argument being made m8. The claim is that link pump > BTC dump - not BTC dump > link dump. If you were contending with the thread seriously, the counterpoint should be that bitcoin dumps when ANYTHING pumps. But that wasn't your counterpoint. Because it doesn't happen. And you know you're being disingenuous.

62264884
>>62264877
you're a poor bagholding loser who has held for 8 years and missed countless opportunities.

62264934
>>62264877
you're a poor bagholding loser who has held for 8 years and missed countless opportunities.

62265147
>>62263870
Sure that's why shit like tron and hype pumped while BTC crashed..kys

62265193
>>62264795

what the fuck are you smoking

62265210
>>62265193

62265234

62265249

62265256

62265265

62265301
>>62264934

Cuckolds can’t accept that their poverty was caused by poor investment decisions rather than a nefarious cabal suppressing their shitcoin kek

62265313
>>62265301

62265377
